addOutpostTooltip: Abandoned outpost — an army and artifact depot that can be captured.
addArenaTooltip: The Gladiator Arena object — required by the arena win mode.
addArenaLimitTooltip: An arena is already placed — the map can have only one.
objectGuardHelp: The object's neutral garrison: strength (0 = unguarded), spawn chance 0–1 and weekly growth. The flag drops the garrison when the object starts owned by a player.
placementHelp: Arguments are stored exactly as in the game format; the presets are the frequent sets from official templates. The first argument anchors to the zone center (per the game code); the exact meaning of the other numbers is undocumented.
placementConnectionMissing: Zone {id}: a main object is placed near a connection that does not exist or does not touch this zone.
placementNearZoneMissing: Zone {id}: a main object is placed near a zone that does not exist.
gladiatorArenaTooMany: The map has {count} arena objects — the game expects exactly one. Remove the extras.
arenaCardHelp: The arena object for the Gladiator Arena mode. By default the generator handles its placement.
gladiatorArenaObjectMissing: The Gladiator Arena mode is enabled, but no arena object is placed in any zone. Add an Arena in the zone inspector.
cityMissingSpawnSource: The castle in zone {id} is not linked to an existing start zone.
cityMissingSpecificFaction: No specific faction is selected for the castle in zone {id}.
newBlankTitle: Clear the design and start with an empty canvas
importDesignTitle: Load a saved editor design working file (.visual-design.json)
exportDesignTitle: Save the editor design with all coordinates and presets to a file (.visual-design.json)
exportTemplateTitle: Export the game-ready .rmg.json. With the map_templates folder picked it writes straight into the game, otherwise it downloads. Shift+click picks/changes the game folder (Chrome/Edge), Ctrl+click forces a download.
exportPngTitle: Export a PNG preview (game style) next to the template, so it shows in the game's template list. Same as the export button: writes into the game folder if picked, otherwise downloads. Shift+click picks the folder, Ctrl+click forces a download.
notificationPngNoZones: Add at least one zone before exporting a preview.
heroRuleFixedSummary: Each player can have at most {max} heroes regardless of castle count.
bansHelp: Banned heroes, spells and items will not appear on the map. Bans are shared by all generation variants.
bansNeedCore: Load Core.zip to pick from the catalog.
bansClearConfirm: Remove all bans? This can be undone (Ctrl+Z).
bonusesHelp: Bonuses every player receives when the match begins. Shared by all generation variants.
bonusesNeedCore: Load Core.zip to pick spells and artifacts from the catalog.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_res: Every player receives the given amount of the resource at the start.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_hero_exp: Starting experience for the receiving hero.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_side_exp: Starting experience for the player (side), not tied to a specific hero.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_hero_spell: The receiving hero knows the chosen spell from the start.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_hero_item: The receiving hero starts the game with this artifact.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_hero_unit: A squad of the chosen creatures joins the receiving hero's starting army.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_hero_unit_multip: The receiving hero's starting army is multiplied by the given factor.
bonusTypeHelp_add_bonus_hero_stat: Changes a stat of the receiving hero: movement bonus, a free spell, or any engine stat set manually.
bonusStatManualHelp: Pick an engine stat and set the increase value (negative values allowed).
bonusStatMovementHelp: Extra movement points per day. For reference: the Seven League Boots grant +30.
singleHeroModeHelp: Each player has exactly one hero for the whole match (the SingleHero game mode). Not to be confused with defeat on losing the starting hero — that is a separate victory setting.
heroHireBanHelp: Heroes cannot be hired in taverns — only the starting heroes play.
expModifiersHelp: Experience multipliers for faction laws and astrology events. 1 = standard, 0.5 = half, 2 = double.
encounterHolesHelp: Some guards are generated so that they can be walked around without a fight.
borderHelp: Map rim: a water band along the perimeter and the rounding of the playfield corners (0–1). Each generation variant has its own value — this edits the active variant.
fixedOrientationHelp: Sets the zero global angle relative to the selected anchor zone and map axes. It does not lock exact coordinates, the direction of a neighboring zone, or prevent RMG from rebuilding parts of the graph. Each generation variant has its own value — this edits the active variant; a new variant allows random rotation by default.
preserveLayoutHelp: Automatically generates invisible layout links (springs) based on zone positions on the canvas. Helps the game generate maps in the desired shape (S-shape, circle, etc.). Each generation variant has its own value — this edits the active variant.
coreRequiredStatus: Load Core.zip to unlock the object catalog and L, M, H, N zone types. Example path: D:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\Heroes of Might and Magic Olden Era\HeroesOldenEra_Data\StreamingAssets\Core.zip
coreRequiredBeforeImport: Load Core.zip first. The editor needs the game catalog to validate and restore objects in the imported design.
coreRequiredValidationText: Load the game archive to unlock the object catalog, zone presets, and design import.
missingPresetObjects: Core.zip is missing {count} preset objects or lists. They will not be added to the template: {items}.
missingImportedObjects: Core.zip is missing {count} objects used by the imported design. They were removed from the template: {items}.
connectHintEmpty: Click two zones to create a link.
saveAsPresetPrompt: Enter a name for the new preset:
confirmPresetChange: Changing the preset will overwrite all current objects and value distributions in this zone. Do you want to proceed?
notificationSpringExists: These zones already have a spring — only one is allowed per pair
notificationAllPlayersHaveSpawns: All players already have starting zones (maximum of {max} players)
startingZoneLimitReachedTooltip: All starting positions for players have already been assigned
emptyInspector: Select a zone or a link on the graph.
zoneBiomeHelp: The biome applies to zone terrain, content, and decorations. The S mode copies the selected start zone's biome.
biomeMissingSpawnSource: Zone {id} references a missing or non-start biome source zone.
biomeOwnWithoutCity: Zone {id} is configured to match its own town, but it has no town.
objectVariantHelp: Numeric ID of a specific object configuration. Auto omits the variant field and leaves the choice to the game generator.
zoneObjectsMandatoryHelp: These are the zone's guaranteed (mandatory) objects — the generator always places them. Random fill comes from the content pools, not from here.
objectNameHelp: Optional name for road-targeting (Mandatory content). Assigned automatically when you first pick this object as a road endpoint; you can change it. Empty = the generator auto-names it on export.
objectOwnerHelp: Player who owns this object from the start (e.g. a mine handed over on turn 1). Neutral = unowned, placed like normal content.
nestedContentHelp: An inline weighted candidate list for this pool slot: which objects may spawn here and how likely each is (weight ≤ 0 = excluded from the roll).
objectUnitIncrementHelp: Tunes this city's army production: the starting growth bump and whether it grows each week. Leave at 0 / off to keep the game default.
objectDesignatedEncounterHelp: Marks this object as a designated encounter for the generator. Leave off unless the original template set it.
objectSoloEncounterHelp: Spawns the object without additional content items around it, making guard strength more predictable. Without this flag it is more likely to appear with other objects, but this is not guaranteed.
objectPlacementHelp: Distance to town targets MainObject 0 in the current zone.
objectTownDistanceUnavailable: Unavailable: this zone has no town (MainObject 0).
objectTownNeedsMainObject: Zone {id} uses an object's town distance, but the zone has no MainObject 0. Add a town or disable this rule.
portalPlacementHelp: Where the portal mouths are placed inside each zone (distance from the zone centre). "Any" lets the generator decide.
connTypeHelpDefault: Standard connection: the generator decides how to lay the passage between the zones.
connTypeHelpDirect: A direct passage between adjacent zones — guarantees a shared border.
connTypeHelpPortal: The zones are linked by a pair of portals; a shared border is not required. Portal placement rules from an imported template are preserved automatically.
connTypeHelpProximity: An invisible "spring": it shapes the relative placement of zones but creates no passage.
connTypeHelpGladiatorArena: A service connection for the Gladiator Arena win condition.
zoneRandomHireHelp: Tuning for the zone's random-hire dwellings, one row per creature tier: starting unit growth and whether it grows weekly. Only shown when the zone uses random-hire content.
zoneRandomHireWeeklyHelp: Weekly unit growth — the dwelling's stock refills every in-game week.
zoneAggressionHelp: How the zone's guards react to a hero: from fleeing or letting pass to always fighting.
zoneAggressionCustomHelp: Six reaction weights (the game's enum order): 1st — always fights (aggressive), 6th — joins or lets pass (docile). The higher a weight, the more likely that reaction.
zoneGuardMainHelp: The multiplier strengthens or weakens every guard in the zone (1 — unchanged). Friendliness (−1…1): the higher, the more eagerly neutrals join or leave without a fight.
zoneGuardExtraHelp: Cutoff: guards weaker than this value are not placed at all. Growth strengthens guards each in-game week (0.15 = +15%), spread is the random strength deviation (0.25 = ±25%).
guardTuningHelp: Growth: how much the guard strengthens each in-game week (0.15 = +15%). Spread: random strength deviation (0.2 = ±20%). 0 keeps the game default.
guardEscapeHelp: When disabled, the guard squad always accepts the fight and never retreats.
edgeGuardZoneHelp: Where the passage guard stands: in the middle or on one zone's side.
edgeGuardMatchGroupHelp: An arbitrary name you make up. Every connection with the same name gets the same generated guard squad. For example, type main_guard on each player's main passage and they all face an identical army. Empty = independent guards.
edgeGatePlacementHelp: Where to place the passage gate. Official templates only use “Center”.
roadTypeHelp: A stone road speeds up movement more than a dirt one.
presetGuardApplyNote: Guard settings are applied to a zone when the preset is selected. Existing zones stay unchanged until the preset is re-applied.
presetRecipeHelp: Role and richness fill the values and guards from official-template statistics; objects are inherited from the base type. Alternatively, clone an existing preset as a whole.
rescaleZoneTooltip: Re-derive the values from the zone's preset and multiply by the area scale (map size ÷ zone count × zone size). Manual value edits get overwritten (Ctrl+Z undoes).
rescaleAllZonesTooltip: Every zone with a preset gets the preset values × the scale of its area. Zones without a preset (imported custom ones) are left alone.
confirmRescaleAllZones: Re-derive all zone values from their presets for the current map size? Manual value edits will be overwritten.
notificationFsUnsupported: This browser cannot save into a folder directly (Chrome or Edge required) — the file will be downloaded instead.
notificationDuplicateSpawnConverted: No free players left (max {max}) — the copied spawn became a city.
notificationNothingToDuplicate: Select a zone or a connection to duplicate.
notificationFsDenied: Folder access was not granted. Use the regular “Export template” or try again.
notificationFsOddFolder: The “{name}” folder holds no .rmg.json files — make sure it is the game's map_templates.
confirmOverwriteGameFile: The file {name} already exists in the game folder. Overwrite? (further saves this session will overwrite it silently)
notificationFsWriteFailed: Could not write the file — the folder is unavailable. Pick it again with the “To game” button.
auxBiomeHelp: The zone's content and large meta-objects can use a biome different from the ground — e.g. the land follows a spawn while the content stays native. They match the land biome by default.
zoneHolesToggle: Custom guard holes for the zone
zoneHolesHelp: Overrides the zone's share of guards that can be walked around (takes effect with the map-level “Guard bypass” setting). Official templates use 0.66/0.66.
overridesDescription: Guard strength overrides for specific objects: wherever the object spawns, the given squad guards it. Official templates use this to reinforce key buildings.
overridesEmpty: No overrides — object guards follow the zone rules.
balanceNoSpawns: No spawn zones — there are no players to compare. The score is not computed.
balanceWealthOk: The players' accessible wealth is symmetric ({spread}% spread).
balanceWealthWarn: The accessible wealth differs between players by {spread}%.
balanceDistanceOk: The players are equally distant from each other.
balanceDistanceBad: Strongly unequal player distances ({spread}%): someone gets noticeably more time to develop.
balanceExitGuardWarn: Player exits are guarded unevenly ({spread}% spread) — someone breaks out earlier.
balanceAdjacentSpawns: Some players sit in adjacent zones — a first-week rush is possible.
balanceSingleExit: {player} has a single exit from the starting zone — easy to lock in.
balanceCheapEntrance: Zone {id}: ~{wealth}K of wealth behind a {guard} entrance. Easy pickings.
balanceSummaryWealth: Wealth: {total}K total, {per}K per player · average passage guard: {guard}
recipesTooltip: Ready-made templates: one click sets the map settings and generates the skeleton, then refine by hand.
recipesDescription: A recipe applies everything at once: map size, modes and the zone skeleton. It is a starting point — everything stays editable. For variations (depth, richness, player count) use the topology wizard.
recipeConfirm: Apply the “{name}” template? The current zones, connections and map settings will be replaced (Ctrl+Z undoes).
recipeDesc_duelTiny: An 80×80 sprint of five zones: the fight starts by the second week.
recipeDesc_duelCrossroads: Two short routes around a rich center on a small map.
recipeDesc_duel: A chain: player — neutrals — player. Poor zones near the start, a rich middle.
recipeDesc_duelMarch: A seven-zone chain: the value grows with every step toward the middle.
recipeDesc_duelJebus: Poor buffers, everything valuable in the huge center — a Jebus Cross race.
recipeDesc_duelAnarchy: Fifteen zones in a random pattern — scouting decides.
recipeDesc_duelEpic: Two deep routes to a grand center on a 192×192 map.
recipeDesc_duelGrand: The largest map: two dozen zones and late-game armies.
recipeDesc_cross: A two-player ring with a fat center — a Jebus-style race for the middle.
recipeDesc_singleHeroDuel: The same duel in single-hero mode: every turn matters.
recipeDesc_ring4: The four-player classic: a ring with player isolation and a central zone.
recipeDesc_star4: All roads lead to the rich center — a constant fight for the middle.
recipeDesc_ffa6: A roomy six-player ring with a center.
recipeDesc_big8: An eight-player ring with deep segments: L–M–H for every player.
recipeDesc_chaos4: A random topology — a new connection pattern every time.
recipeDesc_arena4: A four-player star with the arena object and the arena win mode in the center.
recipeDesc_tournamentDuel: A duel in tournament mode: a scheduled series of fights.
topologyWizardTooltip: Topology wizard: generate a map skeleton (zones and connections) to refine by hand.
topologyHelp_ring: Players evenly on a ring with neutral zones between neighbours. The classic for 2–8 players; optionally a central zone linked to every segment.
topologyHelp_chain: Zones in a line: player — neutrals — player… A duel format, best for 2 players.
topologyHelp_star: A rich center with players around it, each connected by its own spoke of neutrals. All roads lead to the middle.
topologyHelp_random: Players on a circle for fairness, neutrals scattered inside; connections form a shortest skeleton plus a few short extras.
topologyIsolateHelp: No direct player-player passages: early aggression has to fight through neutral zones. When enabled, ring and chain keep at least one neutral between players.
topologyCitiesHelp: Extra neutral cities spread along each player's side, like in the official duels: the first matches the player's faction, the rest are random; guards grow with the distance from the start. Center cities are always random.
topologyNeutralPresets: Neutrals by distance from the players
topologyGradientHelp: The players' neighbours get the “near” preset, the deepest zones the “far” one, everything between “mid”. Distance is counted along the connections, so every player sees the same layout. Tight topologies may have no mid or far zones.
topologyReplaceWarning: Replaces the current zones ({count}) and all connections. Ctrl+Z undoes.
notificationTopologyGenerated: Skeleton generated: {zones} zones, {edges} connections. Values are already scaled to the map.
notificationNothingToRescale: Nothing to rescale: the selected zones have no preset baseline.
roadsEmpty: No internal roads. Add one or use a preset.
roadsHelp: Roads connect endpoints inside the zone for the generator: main objects, the crossroads hub, touching connections. A connection road's surface is mirrored onto that connection.
roadPresetExitHelp: First main object to the first connection.
roadPresetHubHelp: Every main object to the crossroads, the crossroads to every connection.
roadPresetMinesHelp: A dirt road from the crossroads to each named mine object.
roadPresetMinesEmpty: No mine objects in this zone.
roadMandatoryUnnamed: object — gets a name when picked
roadSwap: Reverse the road — swap the from and to endpoints
roadWarnObject: Road {index}: main-object index {ref} is out of range.
roadWarnConnection: Road {index}: connection {ref} does not touch this zone.
zonePerAreaHelp: Extra value from the zone's actual area: total = value × walkable area × 0.1, so bigger zones get richer automatically. 0 — the field is not written to the template. The game's native scaling mechanism, used by a third of official zones.
contentLimitsDescription: Named cap lists: how many instances of an object (or objects from a list) may appear in a zone. The list is shared — editing it affects every zone referencing it.
contentLimitDeleteBlockedShort: The list is referenced by zones — remove the references first
contentLimitBuiltInDeleteBlocked: A built-in list (the zones' Auto option) — cannot be deleted
contentLimitPlayerGate: Apply only for a specific player count
contentLimitUnused: No zone references this list directly yet.
contentLimitRowsEmpty: No rules yet — add objects from the catalog below.
contentLimitMaxCountHelp: The number is the per-zone maximum; 0 bans the object entirely.
contentLimitSearchPlaceholder: Search for an object or list…
notificationContentLimitBuiltIn: The list "{name}" is built-in and cannot be deleted.
notificationContentLimitInUse: The list "{name}" is referenced by zones: {zones}.
zoneContentLimitsHelp: The zone's cap lists: at generation time ONE list is sampled from the set. The lists themselves are edited in the left panel.
zoneContentLimitMissing: Zone {id}: references a missing cap list "{name}".
welcomeCoreText: The full game object catalog — the file lives in the game folder: HeroesOldenEra_Data/StreamingAssets/Core.zip. Parsed locally in your browser.
welcomeRecipesText: A ready map in one click: duel, ring, FFA and other recipes.
welcomeWizardText: A map skeleton from parameters: topology, players, neutral zones.
welcomeImportText: Open an existing .rmg.json template and edit it.
welcomeFootnote: Any starting point can be edited further: move zones, change connections, content and guards.
welcomeManualText: Or by hand: add zones with the preset "+" button on the left, then connect them with the connection tool (key C) on the canvas.
bansApplyStandardTitle: Add the bans the official templates apply most often: voodoo doll, flag of truce, shackles of war, key ring, travel boots and the whole teleport spell family. Current bans are kept.
notificationStandardBansNone: The standard set is already fully banned.
uiModeSimpleTitle: Simple mode: only the basics — zone presets, connections, castles, bans. Format-level knobs are hidden.
uiModeExpertTitle: Expert mode: the full editor — pools, limits, terrain profiles, placement, JSON.
resetBuiltInsTitle: Return the section's built-in entries to factory values (custom entries are untouched; undo with Ctrl+Z)
confirmResetBuiltInPresets: Return the built-in zone presets (S, B, L, M, H, N) to factory values? Custom presets stay untouched; existing zones keep their values.
confirmResetBuiltInTerrain: Return the built-in terrain profiles (visual_editor_layout_*) to factory values? Custom and imported profiles stay untouched.
confirmResetBuiltInLimits: Return the built-in cap lists (content_limits_spawn / content_limits_side) to their factory empty shape? Other lists stay untouched.
notificationBuiltInPresetsReset: Built-in zone presets were reset to factory values.
notificationBuiltInTerrainReset: Built-in terrain profiles were reset to factory values.
notificationBuiltInLimitsReset: Built-in cap lists were reset to factory values.
valueBadgeTitle: Rating relative to the official templates
contentPoolsDescription: Named pools: which object lists (and with what weights) the generator fills a zone from, the price distribution and pool bans. Pools are shared — editing one affects every zone referencing it.
contentPoolsEmpty: No pools yet. Without them zones use the editor's automatic pools (visual_pool_*).
contentPoolDeleteBlockedShort: The pool is referenced by zones — remove the references first
contentPoolUnused: No zone references this pool directly yet.
contentPoolDistributionHelp: Weights control how often the generator picks objects from each price range. The first number of a row is the upper price bound, the second is the weight.
contentPoolGroupsEmpty: No groups yet — add lists from the catalog below.
contentPoolBanSearchPlaceholder: Search for an object to ban…
contentPoolBansHelp: Bans exclude objects from this pool even when they are part of the selected lists.
notificationContentPoolInUse: The pool "{name}" is referenced by zones: {zones}.
zonePoolsHelp: The zone's content pools: at generation time ONE pool is sampled from each set. The pools themselves are edited in the left panel.
poolDistributionInvalid: Pool "{name}": there must be one more weight than price bounds (now {weights} weights and {bounds} bounds) — the game rejects such a pool.
zoneReactionLengthInvalid: Zone {id}: the guard reaction distribution must have exactly 6 weights (now {count}).
terrainProfilesDescription: Named terrain settings (obstacles, lakes, elevation). Profiles are shared: an edit changes the terrain of every zone referencing it.
terrainProfileDeleteBlocked: Cannot delete: the profile is used by zones {zones}. Switch them to another profile first.
terrainProfileUsedBy: Used by zones ({count}): {zones}. Edits affect them all.
terrainObstaclesHelp: Density of forests and rocks (0–1): the first in the zone's main part, the second in unoccupied voids. Official templates use 0.24–0.5.
terrainLakesHelp: Fraction of the zone covered by lakes (0–1, usually up to 0.4) and the minimum lake size in tiles — smaller puddles are dropped.
terrainElevationHelp: How much of the zone is elevated. The generator randomly picks one of the preset's variants on every generation.
terrainScaleHelp: Cluster scale: smaller = finer, more frequent elevation changes. Road cluster area affects how branchy the road net is (official templates use 64–160).
notificationTerrainProfileInUse: Profile {name} is used by zones: {zones}. Deletion is blocked.
notificationTerrainProfileBuiltIn: Profile {name} is built-in — it cannot be deleted.
terrainProfileBuiltInNote: A built-in editor profile: the zones' “Auto” option points to it. The name is fixed, the values are editable; the arrow button restores the factory ones.
confirmResetTerrainProfile: Restore the factory values of profile {name}?
zoneTerrainProfileHelp: The zone's terrain settings: obstacles, lakes, elevation. Profiles are shared — edit them in the left panel, “Terrain profiles” section.
zoneTerrainProfileMissing: Zone {id} references a missing terrain profile “{name}” — the game will fail to generate the map.
springHelp: This connection is invisible in-game and has no guard. The game engine uses it as an invisible spring to align the template layout. Only one spring is allowed between two zones.
routeHelp: Both options create a land passage between the zones; a road makes the route visible and speeds up movement.
routeHelpPortal: When enabled, roads are laid to the portals in both zones.
springWarning: Warning: contradictory or overly tight springs can make the template ungeneratable — the game will fail to place the zones and abort map generation. If your goal is to preserve the map shape, it is safer to enable "Preserve canvas layout in game": the editor builds a consistent set of springs automatically.
emptyCanvas: The canvas is empty. Add zones from the left panel.
heroLimitInvalid: Maximum heroes cannot be lower than starting heroes.
disconnectedGraph: The design is split into {count} disconnected zone groups: {groups}. Every zone must belong to one connected graph.
gladiatorArenaRequiresSpawns: Gladiator Arena requires at least 2 player start zones (spawns).
gladiatorArenaPrepDaysInvalid: Gladiator Arena preparation days must be a positive number.
tournamentRequiresSpawns: Tournament Mode requires at least 2 player start zones (spawns).
tournamentArraysLengthMismatch: Tournament stage durations count must match round announcement days count.
mapSizeInvalid: Map dimensions must be whole numbers of at least 36.
mapSizeNonSquare: The {x} x {z} map is not square. A template with non-standard dimensions may fail to generate in the game.
mapSizeCustom: The {x} x {z} map uses a non-standard size. This template may fail to generate in the game.
rmgGeometryWarning: The game RMG can rotate or mirror a valid zone graph. The editor fixes the graph, not final coordinates.
confirmResetPreset: Reset preset {name} to its default settings?
confirmDeletePreset: Delete custom preset {name}? All zones using it will revert to their base type.
presetObjectsHelp: Click on items in the catalog (left panel) to add them to this preset.
objectTownDistanceUnavailablePreset: Unavailable in preset settings (applies only to S - Spawn).
presetsDescription: Customize standard zone presets (L, M, H, Spawn, Neutral) or create your own custom presets based on them.
noObjects: No objects in this preset. Add them from the catalog.
guardedValueHelp: Total value of objects guarded by monsters in this zone.
unguardedValueHelp: Total value of objects placed without guards in this zone.
resourcesValueHelp: Total value of loose resource piles scattered across this zone.
presetBaseTypeHelp: The archetype the preset is bound to. It drives: the content pools on export (what the game fills the zone with), the spawn machinery (“Spawn” creates a zone with a player castle), the terrain profile in “Auto” mode, the canvas color and the zone name prefix.
classicWinRuleHelp: Win by defeating every enemy hero and capturing every enemy town. This is the base win condition and is usually on alongside the others; turn it off only for a map that wins purely by another condition.
winConditionPresetHelp: One of the game's named win-condition presets. Picking one applies its standard rules and sets the label shown in the game's template picker. The label is cosmetic — the actual rules are the flags; in Expert you can tweak them, which is shown as «modified».
winPreset0Desc: Victory conditions are defined by the map's scenario (standard rules apply by default).
winPreset1Desc: Defeat all enemy heroes and capture every enemy city to win.
winPreset2Desc: Capture the opponent's starting city to win.
winPreset3Desc: Capture the opponent's starting city and hold it for a set number of in-game days to win.
winPreset4Desc: Meet the opponent's hero in a single final battle; the winner claims victory.
winPreset5Desc: Capture the marked neutral city and hold it for a set number of in-game days to win.
winPreset6Desc: Meet the opponent's hero in a series of battles; the winner of the set claims victory.
winPreset7Desc: Capture the opponent's starting city to trigger a final battle with their hero.
presetModifiedNote: The flags below differ from this preset's defaults.
lostStartCityRule: Lose when the starting city is captured
lostStartCityRuleHelp: A player is eliminated when their starting city is taken. Days = 0 means immediately on capture; more than 0 means the attacker must hold it that many in-game days first.
cityHoldRuleHelp: Win by capturing the marked neutral city and holding it for the set number of in-game days. Mark the target city's zone below.
gladiatorArenaRuleHelp: A single decisive arena battle between the players' heroes decides the game.
tournamentRuleHelp: A series of arena battles; the first to the required number of points wins the game.
lostStartHeroDefeat: Defeat on losing the starting hero
lostStartHeroDefeatHelp: A player is eliminated when their starting (main) hero is lost.
desertionRuleHelp: Defeat condition if a player has no castles for a specified number of days and their total army strength is below the threshold.
heroLightingRuleHelp: Reveals the location of the player's remaining heroes on the map if they have no castles for a specified number of days.
victoryCityNotSelected: The victory condition requires holding a neutral city, but no neutral city is selected.
victoryCityNotFound: The selected zone for town hold {id} does not exist.
victoryCityIsSpawn: The selected zone {id} is a start zone. Select a neutral zone for neutral city hold.
victoryCityNoCity: The selected zone {id} does not contain a castle. Enable castle in this zone's settings.
gladiatorRegStartFightHelp: If enabled, the duel starts immediately after registration ends without additional delay days.
tournamentStageDaysHelp: Duration of each tournament round in days. The number of values must match the count of rounds.
tournamentAnnounceDaysHelp: In-game days when each round is announced. The number of values must match the count of stage durations.
customListNestedNotFound: Nested list {nestedId} inside custom list {listId} does not exist
emptyCustomListTip: Select objects in the left panel and click '+' to add them here.
customListObjectNotFound: Object {sid} inside custom list {listId} not found in catalog
notificationCustomListIdConflict: ID '{id}' is already taken by another object, preset, or set
detachOriginalLayoutTooltip: Detach from original. Resets original zone layout geometry and unlocks automatic spring physics recalculation on export.
confirmDetachOriginalLayout: Are you sure you want to detach this template from the original? This will reset the original layout geometry and unlock automatic coordinates-based generation. This action is irreversible (original fine-tuned layout and springs settings will be permanently lost). Do you want to proceed?
showAllElementsTooltip: Show all design elements (list of zones and connections)
variantsHelp: Each variant is an alternative map layout. The settings in the Map block above are shared by all variants; only zones and connections are per-variant.
